sections.title: A: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each of the numbered words or phrases in bold is incorrect. Rewrite them correctly. Write - if the bold word or phrase simply needs to be removed.

items.sentence: Renewable energy might help **to us** cut our carbon footprint, but imagine if we could completely **out** eliminate fossil fuels and stop pollution from **in** the first place.

Reversing climate change may be the greatest scientific challenge of our era. For a **long** of time, experts have debated whether global action is even possible, but reaching a binding international agreement has proved far harder than they expected **that**.
The global climate system is incredibly complex, with rising temperatures having a direct impact **taken** on ecosystems all over the world.
The researchers may feel they are wasting **up** valuable resources, but a breakthrough could transform the way we understand our planet.

sections.title: B: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

items.sentence: The weatherman said there was a high (LIKELY) ...... of storms this weekend.
Everyone knows that driving cars produces (POLLUTE) ......, yet millions of us still choose to drive every day.
Predicting the effects of climate change (ACCURATE) ...... requires a great deal of scientific expertise.
Noise pollution is often a serious problem in (RESIDENT) ...... neighbourhoods near busy roads.
According to leading (ENVIRONMENT) ......, we may be approaching an irreversible tipping point.
It was a gloriously (SUN) ...... morning, so we decided to have breakfast outside in the garden.
The Bengal tiger is (DANGER) ...... because deforestation is rapidly destroying its natural habitat.
We didn't stay long at the viewpoint because it was absolutely (FREEZE) ...... up there!

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
Did you see the nature documentary about the Amazon rainforest? **saw**

Zoe put on her wellies and, the moment the rain stopped, dashed outside to jump in the .......
